staffRoles.RoleDescription: The one who installs the software
Has access to Analytics including Session and daily Reports
Front Desk admin or a Receptionist and does billing, but doesn't have permission to register new staff.
Application Admin who registers the staffs and modifies the master data

scanningtemplate.Name: ABD-M-Normal
scanningtemplate.ScanTemplateData:
ULTRASOUND SCANNING OF ABDOMEN AND PELVIS

Liver: Normal in shape, position and size. No obvious hypo/hyperechoic focus noted within the liver. Hepatic veins and intrahepatic IVC are normal. No intra / extrahepatic biliary dilation. C.B.D. Normal. Portal vein- Normal.

Gall Bladder: Well distended and shows echofree lumen. No calculus / sludge. Wall thickness normal. No pericholecystic collection.

Spleen: Normal in location, echopattern and size.

Pancreas: Head and proximal body is normal in shape and echopattern. Distal body and tail of pancreas not evaluated due to bowel shadows.

KIDNEYS:

                Bipolar Measurements       Parenchymal thickness

Right Kidney :           cms        x       cms

Left  Kidney :           cms         x     cms

Both kidneys are normal in size, shape and location. Corticomedullary differentiation well maintained. No evidence of renal calculi / Hydroureteronephrosis in either kidney. No free fluid noted in abdomen.

Urinary Bladder: is well distended. No calculus / diverticulum. Wall thickness normal.

Prostate: Normal in size and echotexture Volume: cc

IMPRESSION:

Essentially Normal Study

 

Dr.N.Diwakar DMRD DNB

Consultant Radiologist

scanningtemplate.Name: DF-16
scanningtemplate.ScanTemplateData:
Pelvic Scan Report

Clinical Indication: Routine checkup

Observations:

  • Uterus: Normal size and shape.
  • Endometrium: 8mm, regular.
  • Ovaries: Both ovaries appear normal in size and echotexture.
  • No free fluid in POD.

Impression: Normal pelvic study.

scanningitem.Title: LMP
scanningitem.Description: Not known
Gravid anteverted measures 10.7 X 7.0 X 8.5 cms
Anterior wall subserosal fibroid seen in lower uterine segment measuring 30 x 35 mm. Posterior wall intramural fibroid seen measuring 16X12.9 mm
Single intrauterine gestation sac seen , with single viable fetal pole
CRL-- 3.5 cms corresponds to 10 weeks 3 days
Fetal heart activity noted with FHR -160 B/min
Fetal spontaneous movements good.
Placenta implanted anterior and inferior covering internal OS partially. No evidence of retro chorionic bleed/clot.

scanningitem.Title: Right Ovary
scanningitem.Description: X     cms

scanningitem.Title: Left Ovary
scanningitem.Description: X   cms
Both Ovaries are normal in size, shape and echotexture.
No adnexal mass.

scanningitem.Title: POD
scanningitem.Description: No fluid seen
Impression
- Single intrauterine gestation sac seen with single viable fetal pole of about 10 weeks 3 days gestation
-No evidence of retrochorionic beed/clot
Placenta implanted anterior and inferiro covering internal OS partially
Uterine fibroids seen as described above
Suggested NT scan at 12-14 Weeks and anomaly scan at 22 weeks gestation

Dr.      declare that while conducting ultrasonography/scanning on     
I have neither detected nor disclosed and sex of her foetus to any body in any manner.
DECLARARTION OF DCOCTOR/PERSON CONDUCTING ULTRASONOGRAPHY/IMAGE SCANNING
HSG Films taken after injecting contrast into uterine cavity
Uterine cavity appears normal
Bilaterally the fallopian tubes are normal in size and contour
Billaterally spillage of contrast noted into the peritoneal cavity
Few Well-defined round to oval shaped filling deffects are seen in the endometrial  cavity-artifactual due to? Air bubbles. Suggested to Correlate with USG findings.
IMPRESSION:
-Normal Hyterosalphingogram study
Bilateral patent fallopian tubes

surgery.Name: Tonsillectomy
surgery.Description: Surgical removal of the tonsils, typically due to chronic tonsillitis or sleep apnea.

surgery.Name: Tonsilectomy + Adenoidectomy
surgery.Description: Combined removal of tonsils and adenoids to treat recurring infections or airway obstruction.

surgery.Name: Adenotonsillectomy
surgery.Description: A single procedure to remove both the adenoids and the tonsils, common in pediatric ENT patients.

surgery.Name: Tympanoplasty
surgery.Description: Reconstructive surgery for a perforated eardrum to restore hearing and prevent infection.

surgery.Name: Mastoidectomy
surgery.Description: Removal of diseased mastoid air cells in the hollow, air-filled bone behind the ear.

surgery.Name: Fess Bilateral
surgery.Description: Functional Endoscopic Sinus Surgery on both sides to treat chronic sinusitis and nasal polyps.

surgery.Name: Mastoidectomy & Tympanoplasty
surgery.Description: Combined surgery to clear mastoid infection and reconstruct the eardrum and middle ear bones.

surgery.Name: Peritonsilar Abscess Drainage (DAY CARE)
surgery.Description: Incision and drainage of an abscess located near the tonsils, performed as a day care procedure.

surgery.Name: Haemorrhoidectomy
surgery.Description: Surgical procedure to remove internal or external hemorrhoids that are extensive or severe.

surgery.Name: Haemorrhoidectomy + Fissurectomy
surgery.Description: Concurrent removal of hemorrhoids and an anal fissure to address complex anorectal issues.

surgery.Name: Fissurectomy & Fissure Dilatation
surgery.Description: Removal of an anal fissure followed by controlled stretching of the anal canal to promote healing.

surgery.Name: High End Fissurectomy
surgery.Description: Advanced surgical excision of an anal fissure located higher in the anal canal.

surgery.Name: Low End Fissurectomy
surgery.Description: Surgical excision of an anal fissure located near the anal verge.

surgery.Name: Appendectomy (LAP)
surgery.Description: Laparoscopic removal of the appendix, a minimally invasive alternative to open surgery.

surgery.Name: Appendectomy (open)
surgery.Description: Traditional surgical removal of the appendix through a single larger incision.

surgery.Name: Cholesystectomy (LAP)
surgery.Description: Minimally invasive laparoscopic surgery to remove the gallbladder, usually for gallstones.

surgery.Name: Mastectomy (Simple)
surgery.Description: Removal of the entire breast tissue, but not the lymph nodes or underlying muscle.

surgery.Name: Mastectomy (Radical)
surgery.Description: Extensive removal of the breast, underlying chest muscles, and lymph nodes for advanced cancer.

surgery.Name: I & D
surgery.Description: Incision and Drainage of a skin abscess or boil to relieve pain and pressure.

surgery.Name: Thyroidectomy (Total / Sub Total)
surgery.Description: Complete or partial removal of the thyroid gland to treat nodules, cancer, or hyperthyroidism.

surgery.Name: Inguinal Hernioplasty-Unilateral Open
surgery.Description: Open surgical repair of a groin hernia on one side using a mesh for reinforcement.

surgery.Name: Inguinal Hernioplasty-Unilateral  Lap
surgery.Description: Laparoscopic repair of a groin hernia on one side, offering faster recovery times.

surgery.Name: Umblical Hernioplasty Open
surgery.Description: Open surgical repair of an umbilical (belly button) hernia.

surgery.Name: Umblical Hernioplasty  Lap
surgery.Description: Minimally invasive laparoscopic repair of an umbilical hernia.

surgery.Name: Incisional Hernioplasty  Open
surgery.Description: Open repair of a hernia that has developed at the site of a previous surgical incision.

surgery.Name: Incisional Hernioplasty  Lap
surgery.Description: Laparoscopic repair of a hernia occurring at a previous incision site.

surgery.Name: Circumcision (DAY CARE)
surgery.Description: Surgical removal of the foreskin from the penis, performed as a day care procedure.

surgery.Name: Perianal Abscess
surgery.Description: Surgical drainage of a collection of pus located in the tissues surrounding the anus.

surgery.Name: Breast Lumpectomy
surgery.Description: Surgical removal of a discrete lump from the breast, preserving the rest of the breast tissue.

surgery.Name: Hydrocele
surgery.Description: Surgical correction of a fluid-filled sac surrounding a testicle that causes swelling in the scrotum.

surgery.Name: Exploratory Laparotomy
surgery.Description: Large surgical incision through the abdominal wall to gain access to the abdominal cavity for diagnosis.

surgery.Name: Normal Delivery
surgery.Description: Standard vaginal childbirth without significant complications or surgical intervention.

surgery.Name: Normal delivery with Epidural
surgery.Description: Vaginal childbirth assisted by epidural anesthesia for pain management.

surgery.Name: Complicated Normal Delivery
surgery.Description: Vaginal delivery involving unexpected medical issues that require specialized care.

surgery.Name: Instrumental /Assisted Delivery
surgery.Description: Childbirth using forceps or vacuum extraction to assist the passage of the baby.

surgery.Name: LSCS
surgery.Description: Lower Segment Caesarean Section; surgical delivery of a baby through an abdominal and uterine incision.

surgery.Name: LSCS for Twins
surgery.Description: Caesarean section specifically performed for the delivery of twins.

surgery.Name: Complicated LSCS
surgery.Description: C-section delivery involving high-risk factors or maternal/fetal complications.

surgery.Name: Hysterectomy LAP LAVH
surgery.Description: Laparoscopic-Assisted Vaginal Hysterectomy; removal of the uterus using laparoscopy.

surgery.Name: Hysterectomy Open
surgery.Description: Traditional abdominal surgery to remove the uterus through a single large incision.

surgery.Name: Myomectomy - LAP
surgery.Description: Laparoscopic surgical removal of uterine fibroids while preserving the uterus.

surgery.Name: Myomectomy - open
surgery.Description: Surgical excision of uterine fibroids through an open abdominal incision.

surgery.Name: TAH + BSO + Adhesiolysis
surgery.Description: Total Abdominal Hysterectomy, Bilateral Salpingo-Oophorectomy, and removal of internal adhesions.

surgery.Name: Ectopic Pregnancy Open
surgery.Description: Open surgical removal of an embryo growing outside the uterus, usually in a fallopian tube.

surgery.Name: Ectopic Pregnancy Lap
surgery.Description: Laparoscopic removal of an ectopic pregnancy, reducing surgical trauma.

surgery.Name: Ovarian Cystectomy
surgery.Description: Surgical removal of a cyst from an ovary while leaving the rest of the ovary intact.

surgery.Name: Dilatation & Curettage (D&C)
surgery.Description: Procedure to scrape the uterine lining for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.

surgery.Name: Total Knee Replacement-Unilateral
surgery.Description: Surgical replacement of a single damaged knee joint with an artificial prosthesis.

surgery.Name: Total Knee Replacement-Bilateral
surgery.Description: Concurrent surgical replacement of both knee joints with artificial prostheses.

surgery.Name: Hip Replacement Unilateral
surgery.Description: Surgical removal of a diseased hip joint and replacement with an artificial joint.

surgery.Name: Fracture Neck Femur
surgery.Description: Surgical repair or replacement of the femoral neck following a break near the hip joint.

surgery.Name: Femur shaft fracture- Proximal/Middle/Distal
surgery.Description: Internal fixation of the femur shaft using rods, plates, or screws to stabilize a break.

surgery.Name: Tiba Fracture Proximal/Middle/Distal/Orif/Crif with Nailing/Lock
surgery.Description: Stabilization of a tibia break using internal nailing, locking plates, or external fixation.

surgery.Name: Foot Fractures - With Wires
surgery.Description: Fixation of small bones in the foot using surgical K-wires for stability during healing.

surgery.Name: Calcaneal Fracture with Plates
surgery.Description: Surgical stabilization of a heel bone (calcaneus) fracture using plates and screws.

surgery.Name: Shoulder/Humerous/Elbow/Both bones/Forarn fractures-Plates ANA
surgery.Description: Open reduction and internal fixation of upper limb fractures using anatomical plates.

surgery.Name: Single Forarm Fracture/ Wrist Fracture with plate
surgery.Description: Surgical repair of a radius or ulna fracture using a compression plate.

surgery.Name: Scaphiod Fracture Fixation
surgery.Description: Surgical repair of a scaphoid bone fracture in the wrist, often using a headless compression screw.

surgery.Name: Hand- "K" Wires- Single Fracture
surgery.Description: Stabilization of a finger or hand fracture using temporary surgical wires.

surgery.Name: Menisectomy
surgery.Description: Arthroscopic removal of a portion of torn meniscus cartilage in the knee.

surgery.Name: Arthroscopic Surgery
surgery.Description: Minimally invasive joint surgery performed through small incisions using a camera.

surgery.Name: SEShoulder -Arthroscopy/ Open -Laterjet/ Bone Block
surgery.Description: Advanced surgery for shoulder instability, involving bone transfer to prevent dislocation.

surgery.Name: Shoulder -Arthroscopy/ Open-Cuff Repair with Anchors
surgery.Description: Repair of a torn rotator cuff using surgical anchors to reattach the tendon to the bone.

surgery.Name: Shoulder- Arthroscopy / Open-Sub Acromial Decompression
surgery.Description: Procedure to increase the space in the shoulder joint by removing bone spurs or inflamed tissue.

surgery.Name: ACL Reconstruction / Repair
surgery.Description: Surgical replacement of a torn Anterior Cruciate Ligament with a tissue graft to restore knee stability.

surgery.Name: MCI. Reconstruction / Repiar
surgery.Description: Surgical stabilization of a torn Medial Collateral Ligament in the knee.

surgery.Name: ACI. & PCL Reconstruction and Repair
surgery.Description: Complex surgery to repair multiple major knee ligaments (ACL and PCL) simultaneously.

surgery.Name: Carpel Tunnel Release - Bilateral
surgery.Description: Surgery to relieve pressure on the median nerve in both wrists by cutting the carpal ligament.

surgery.Name: Close reduction of fracture of Limbs and POP
surgery.Description: Non-surgical alignment of a broken bone followed by application of a Plaster of Paris (POP) cast.

surgery.Name: implant Removal for Small Bones
surgery.Description: Surgical removal of pins, wires, or plates from hand, foot, or wrist bones after healing.

surgery.Name: Implant Removal (Femur / TIBIA)
surgery.Description: Surgical extraction of large intramedullary nails or plates from the leg bones.

surgery.Name: PCNL - Unilateral
surgery.Description: Percutaneous Nephrolithotomy; minimally invasive surgery to remove large kidney stones.

surgery.Name: Pyeloplasty Open Unilateral
surgery.Description: Open surgery to clear a blockage where the kidney meets the ureter (UPJ obstruction).

surgery.Name: Pyeloplasty Lap Unilateral
surgery.Description: Laparoscopic repair of a blockage in the ureteric outflow system.

surgery.Name: TURP
surgery.Description: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate; surgery to treat an enlarged prostate by removing tissue through the urethra.

surgery.Name: DJ Stent Removal
surgery.Description: Endoscopic removal of a temporary ureteral stent previously placed to maintain urine flow.

surgery.Name: Cystoscopy
surgery.Description: Examination of the bladder lining and urethra using a camera-equipped tube.

surgery.Name: Cystoscopy, URS with DJ Stenting
surgery.Description: Comprehensive endoscopic check followed by ureteroscopy and stent placement to treat stones.

surgery.Name: ESWL (DAY CARE)
surgery.Description: Extracorporeal Shock Wave Lithotripsy; non-invasive treatment of kidney stones using sound waves.

surgery.Name: URS (Therapeutic) - Unilateral
surgery.Description: Therapeutic Ureteroscopy to remove or fragment a ureteral stone on one side.

surgery.Name: Varicose Veins
surgery.Description: Surgical removal or closure of enlarged, twisted veins, usually in the legs.

housekeeping_tasks.Notes: Need to be done by 11 Am
